desires: Not let the cohort down tonight by showing up exhausted and unprepared for our review session.
Get this midterm prep done before midnight so I can actually go out with my friends without feeling like a failure tomorrow.
Push back on anyone trying to drag me into late-night cramming if it means sacrificing sleep, because I won't crash until the work is absolutely done.

goals.description: Send the boundary text to Mina immediately: 'Midterm is my priority tonight. I'll be at the club by 23:30 sharp. If I'm late or missing, it's because of the exam, not because I don't care about you.' Do not wait for her to ask.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive screams that if I show up and can't deliver on the 'courage' to study tomorrow, they'll see me as weak. But my assertiveness means I won't just say 'I'm here'; I'll demand a boundary so we don't all burn out together. It's not about quitting; it's about setting terms so the hangout doesn't become a tragedy of wasted time.

goals.description: Sprint through the last 20 minutes of review now to secure the 'responsible' look before the party starts, then immediately drop everything to join the cohort.
goals.rationale: I dropped this because my sleep debt (2 hours) and low stamina (6) mean grinding right now will only make me crash harder later. My impulsive nature wants to feel productive *now*, but doing deep work while exhausted feels like a betrayal of my own health needs. I'll do this after the social connection is secured, or skip it entirely to preserve energy for the party where relationships actually matter.

goals.description: Run a load of laundry tonight so I don't have to deal with it first thing when I crash.
goals.rationale: This is the compromise. Doing laundry *after* the group has left preserves our social bond (my top value) while still clearing the immediate clutter. If I leave it for Sunday, I risk waking up in a panic. If I do it before the group, I lose the night. Doing it now, right after the party ends, satisfies the task without sacrificing the people who matter most.

agent.last_reasoning: Affiliation demands I secure Mina's trust before the night gets messy. My assertiveness means I won't wait for an apology; I'll set the boundary now to protect our bond from future resentment. Skipping study until after ensures I can actually show up without burning out later.

goals.description: Finish the final 2 hours of review, then immediately join the cohort at Echo.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is maxed out; skipping tonight because I'm 'barely done' would be a massive betrayal to my close friends (closeness: 7). The guilt of procrastination tomorrow is too heavy to carry if I can just finish this quick block now. I won't let them down.

goals.description: Arrive at Echo by 20:30 sharp and stay until the group leaves.
goals.rationale: Once I get there, socializing takes precedence over studying or laundry. My assertiveness means I'll own my presence without apologizing for being late, but the primary drive right now is securing that social slot.

goals.description: Drop all other study tasks and call it a night.
goals.rationale: I've already prioritized the hangout. Pushing myself further risks burnout before the party even starts. If I miss the vibe, I can always come back to the notes later, but tonight's connection is non-negotiable.

desires: Not let the whole cohort down by skipping tonight's hangout, even though I'm barely done with review.
Get through this last quick block of studying so I can actually go out without looking like a pushover.
Avoid the guilt of staying up late again tomorrow because of my own laziness.
